concerns.text: T012 and T013 are marked [P] (parallel-safe) but both target the same file `models/episodic_memory.py`. Implementing the class structure (T012) and the embedding generation logic (T013) in the same file creates a hidden shared-state dependency; they cannot be safely merged or executed in parallel without merge conflicts or partial-state errors. The [P] tag is semantically incorrect for tasks sharing a single output file.

T020 (Implement PlanningService) and T024 (Integrate with US1 components) both target `services/planning_service.py`. T024 explicitly depends on the completion of T020 to have a service to 'integrate with'. Marking T024 as [P] (parallel) alongside T020 violates the producer-consumer rule; the integration logic cannot exist before the service skeleton is defined.

T014 (Implement RetrievalService) is the producer of the service logic. T015 (Implement fallback logic) and T016 (Add validation) are consumers that modify the same file `services/retrieval_service.py`. While T015/16 depend on T014, they are also marked [P] in the list (implied by grouping) or lack explicit sequential ordering. T015 and T016 should not be parallel to T014 as they require the base service to exist first.

T010 and T011 are marked [P] and placed before implementation tasks (T012+). While TDD (Test-Driven Development) is a valid strategy, the tasks are described as 'Unit test for... storage logic' and 'Integration test for...'. If the implementation (T012) does not exist, these tests will fail to import the module. The [P] tag is valid for the *writing* of tests, but the dependency on the *existence* of the module (even if empty) should be noted, or the tasks should be explicitly sequenced as 'Write test stubs' -> 'Implement' -> 'Refactor tests'.
